Keyboard Shortcuts.details: Completes the name of a cmdlet, parameter, or file path based on the partial input.

Keyboard Shortcuts.desc: View command history in a UI panel
Keyboard Shortcuts.details: Opens a small overlay window that lists recently executed commands for easy selection.
Allows you to navigate through previous commands one by one in the terminal.

Keyboard Shortcuts.desc: Interrupt or cancel the currently running command
Keyboard Shortcuts.details: Sends an interrupt signal to the active process, stopping it immediately.
Clears all text from the current terminal buffer, providing a clean workspace.

Keyboard Shortcuts.desc: Delete from the cursor to the start of the line
Keyboard Shortcuts.details: Quickly removes all characters between the current cursor position and the beginning of the input line.

Keyboard Shortcuts.desc: Delete from the cursor to the end of the line
Keyboard Shortcuts.details: Quickly removes all characters between the current cursor position and the end of the input line.

Keyboard Shortcuts.desc: Move the cursor one word at a time
Keyboard Shortcuts.details: Jumps the cursor over entire words instead of single characters for faster navigation.
Erases the list of commands you've typed during the current session.
Deletes the entire line of text you are currently typing.

Help & Discovery.desc: Show help for a specific command
Help & Discovery.details: Displays the documentation, syntax, and description for a PowerShell cmdlet.

Help & Discovery.desc: Show examples for a specific command
Help & Discovery.details: Filters the help documentation to show only the usage examples for the cmdlet.

Help & Discovery.desc: List all available cmdlets, aliases, and functions
Help & Discovery.details: Retrieves a comprehensive list of all commands installed on the system.

Help & Discovery.desc: List all command aliases (e.g., ls -> Get-ChildItem)
Help & Discovery.details: Shows the mapping between short alias names and their full PowerShell cmdlet names.

Help & Discovery.desc: Show properties and methods of an object
Help & Discovery.details: Inspects an object to reveal its available attributes (properties) and actions (methods).

File & Directory Navigation.details: Retrieves the files and subdirectories in one or more specified locations.
Sets the current working directory to the specified path.

File & Directory Navigation.desc: Print the current working directory (Alias: pwd)
File & Directory Navigation.details: Displays the path of the directory you are currently working in.

File & Directory Navigation.desc: Create a new file or directory (Alias: ni)
File & Directory Navigation.details: Creates a new item, such as a file or folder, at the specified path.

File & Directory Navigation.desc: Delete a file or directory (Alias: rm, del)
File & Directory Navigation.details: Deletes one or more items, such as files, folders, or registry keys.

File & Directory Navigation.desc: Copy files or directories (Alias: cp, copy)
File & Directory Navigation.details: Duplicates an item from one location to another.

File & Directory Navigation.desc: Move or rename files or directories (Alias: mv, move)
File & Directory Navigation.details: Transfers an item to a new location or changes its name.

File & Directory Navigation.desc: Check if a file or directory exists
File & Directory Navigation.details: Returns True if all elements of the path exist, and False if any are missing.

File Content & Text.desc: Read and output file contents (Alias: cat)
File Content & Text.details: Retrieves the content of an item, such as the text in a file.

File Content & Text.desc: Write text to a file (overwrites existing content)
File Content & Text.details: Writes or replaces the content of an item with the specified new content.
Adds content to an existing item without overwriting its current contents.

File Content & Text.desc: Search for text within files (Alias: grep)
File Content & Text.details: Finds text patterns in strings and files using regular expression matching.

System & Processes.details: Gets the processes that are running on the local or a remote computer.

System & Processes.desc: Kill a process by name (Alias: kill)
System & Processes.details: Stops one or more running processes based on their name or ID.

System & Processes.desc: Start a new process or program
System & Processes.details: Launches one or more processes, such as a program or a document file.
Gets the services on a local or remote computer, including their status.
Stops and then starts one or more services.

Networking.details: Sends ICMP echo request packets to one or more computers to check connectivity.
Resolves a DNS name to obtain its IP address or other DNS record information.
Retrieves the IP address configuration for the network interfaces on the computer.

Networking.desc: Make an HTTP request (Alias: curl, wget)
Networking.details: Gets content from a web page on the internet for processing or scraping.
